news.title: Mysuru development body plans to raise ₹1,000 crore by auctioning vacant plots online
news.para: Mysuru Development Authority aims to raise Rs 1,000 crore by auctioning 150 acres of vacant land. This initiative seeks to fund new projects, including layout development and housing, while preventing land encroachment. The auction will be conducted online, offering developed plots of various sizes to ensure better land utilization and financial stability for MDA.

news.title: West Bengal drafts plan for reuse of treated wastewater in housing units
news.para: West Bengal's urban development department has drafted a policy promoting safe reuse of treated wastewater for non-potable purposes. The policy aims to reuse at least 20% of treated wastewater by 2030 across various sectors, including industry, agriculture, and housing. It also suggests imposing user charges and commercializing treated water use for revenue generation.

news.title: About 195% more groundwater drawn by Gurugram than it replenished last year
news.para: Gurgaon's groundwater extraction is alarmingly high, exceeding permissible limits by 194.6%, a negligible improvement from last year. This over-exploitation, driven by urbanisation and industrial demand, depletes aquifers faster than they can replenish. Despite warnings, the city relies heavily on groundwater, highlighting a critical need for sustainable water management and increased rainwater harvesting.

news.title: Residents in Mohali protest over delay in PR-6 road construction
news.para: Residents protested a private builder's alleged role in delaying the PR-6 road construction in Mohali. The project, crucial for connectivity, is stalled due to a legal dispute over land compensation, impacting thousands of residents. Puda officials stated the case is sub judice and concerns public interest.

news.title: IKEA aims to be profitable by FY28; to open 25 small & medium stores in next 4-5 years
news.para: Swedish furniture giant IKEA is set to become profitable in India by FY28. The company plans to open 25 new stores across the country in the next four to five years. This expansion will focus on smaller formats to reach more customers. IKEA is also enhancing its operational efficiency and local sourcing to drive growth and improve margins.

news.title: ICICI Prudential AIF acquires Pune IT park asset for about ₹520 crore
news.para: An investment fund managed by ICICI Prudential Asset Management has bought a large office space in Pune. This acquisition is part of a strategy to build a portfolio of income-generating commercial real estate. The fund aims to provide investors access to prime office assets. This move taps into India's growing commercial real estate market.

news.title: US-based Charles Schwab leases 3.45 lakh sq ft office space in Hyderabad for GCC
news.para: Charles Schwab is expanding its global operations in India. The US financial services firm has leased a significant office space in Hyderabad's Gachibowli. This move highlights Hyderabad's growing importance as a hub for multinational companies. The lease agreement includes phased commencement and rental escalations. This expansion is expected to boost the city's office market.

news.title: DDA to take over day-to-day upkeep of flats from April
news.para: Delhi Development Authority will now manage day-to-day maintenance of its housing complexes, including pre-2021 flats, to boost sales. Flat owners will pay quarterly charges for services like security, cleaning, and minor repairs. This initiative aims to ensure efficient upkeep until occupancy reaches 80% or an RWA is registered.

news.title: Hutment sale scam returns to haunt HDIL
news.para: Mumbai Police's EOW has uncovered a Rs 150 crore housing scam involving HDIL Group, where possession documents for 1,000 slum rehab residents were allegedly sold to third parties. Former staffer's statement points to HDIL promoters, the Wadhwans, selling these files for substantial sums, leading to unauthorized occupants in prime transit housing.

news.title: Residential colony comes up on lake, NGT asks Ghaziabad DM to clear plot
news.para: The National Green Tribunal has ordered the removal of encroachments on a lake in Pasunda village, Loni. Land records confirm survey number 1564 is a waterbody. A residential colony was being developed on this land. The tribunal will continue its hearing on environmental aspects. Further proceedings are scheduled for March 24.

news.title: Housing board offers discounts on Siddharth Vihar plots & flats in Ghaziabad
news.para: UP Housing Board is offering a festive season rebate of up to 15% on plots and residential units in its Siddharth Vihar township until March 31. Located strategically along the Delhi-Meerut Expressway, the township is a 5-minute drive from Ghaziabad rapid rail and Shaheed Sthal metro stations.

news.title: Karnataka to roll out AI-based Kaveri 3.0 registration system, GIS land stack under Budget 2026
news.para: Karnataka's Chief Minister has unveiled a budget focused on modernising land records and property registration. New systems will streamline processes and enhance transparency. Bengaluru will see significant infrastructure upgrades, including road development and stormwater drain improvements. The state also plans to build more houses, increasing subsidies for beneficiaries.

news.title: Nayanthara buys duplex apartment in Chennai's Poes Garden for ₹31.5 crore
news.para: South Indian star Nayanthara and husband Vignesh Shivan have purchased a luxurious duplex in Chennai's exclusive Poes Garden for Rs 31.5 crore. The sprawling 14,369 sq ft property, located in the Legacy project, underscores the enclave's status as a prime address for celebrities and high-net-worth individuals, with property values consistently reaching crores.

news.title: Ludhiana development body issues notices to two builders over unauthorized constructions
news.para: Glada has initiated a zero-tolerance policy against unauthorized construction, issuing notices for deviations from approved building plans. This crackdown includes actions against commercial activity beyond sanctioned limits and significant structural violations. Residents are warned that non-compliance will lead to plan cancellation, sealing, or demolition, with full responsibility falling on owners.

news.title: Pay 11% interest from March 2021 till flat handover: Haryana RERA to Burman Estate
news.para: Haryana Rera has ordered Burman Estate Pvt Ltd to pay delay possession charges to two allottees for their serviced apartment in Gurgaon Spectrum Centre. The developer failed to offer possession by the stipulated March 15, 2021 deadline, despite the Occupation Certificate being issued. Rera rejected the developer's plea for a second Covid-19 related extension.

news.title: Administration reclaims Nazul land in Kanpur after illegal sale
news.para: Kanpur District Magistrate has ordered government re-entry into a valuable Nazul property in Civil Lines, valued at Rs 24.77 crore. The land was illegally sold to a private builder in 2012 by heirs who concealed facts about the lease. The sale occurred despite non-payment of lease rent and lease renewal, violating Nazul Manual provisions.

news.title: Telangana RERA allows homebuyers to finish stalled project
news.para: Telangana authorities have empowered homebuyers to complete stalled housing projects after a builder's default, a first under the RERA Act. Flat owners formed associations to finish construction on two projects by Jayathri Infrastructures. This move safeguards investments and ensures project completion, with one project already finished and another underway.

news.title: Uttar Pradesh: Water bodies must in real estate projects spanning 10 acres
news.para: New regulations mandate that real estate projects over 10 acres must allocate 1% of land for waterbodies to recharge groundwater and recycle water for non-potable uses. This initiative aims to combat urban runoff and the 'urban island effect'. Additionally, developers must minimize concrete in green spaces and use permeable materials for jogging tracks and walkways, ensuring water seepage.

news.title: Maharashtra CM announces ₹23,487-crore metro line to Gateway of India, underground tunnel network
news.para: Maharashtra Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis unveiled ambitious infrastructure plans. The Budget 2026-27 includes extensive underground tunnel networks in Mumbai and Pune. A Rs 23,487 crore metro line to the Gateway of India is also planned. The state aims for 1,200 km of metro lines and over 6,000 km of expressways by 2047, enhancing connectivity for passengers and freight.

news.title: House prices in UK rise but inflation fears cloud outlook
news.para: In February, UK house prices experienced their quickest surge since October, signaling a vibrant market. Yet, rising mortgage rates are casting a shadow, fueled by inflationary concerns linked to tensions in the Middle East. Consequently, financial markets anticipate a reduced likelihood of interest rate reductions from the Bank of England, placing additional strain on housing affordability for prospective buyers.
